text.title: Service Delivery SOP Template Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding Service Delivery SOP Template. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every SOP step MUST declare its named inputs (artefact/data) AND where those inputs come from. Implicit inputs forbidden.
rule.rationale: Implicit inputs cause first-execution failures: contractor pings founder mid-step, defeating delegation.

rule.statement: Every 'it depends' moment in the source service MUST be formalised as a decision step with explicit branch conditions evaluable without founder input.
rule.rationale: Implicit branches encode founder expertise; contractor breaks on first edge case.

rule.statement: Every step MUST define at least one quality gate: pass/fail check on the operator's own work before proceeding.
rule.rationale: Without per-step gates, defects propagate downstream; rework cost compounds.

rule.statement: Every decision step AND quality gate MUST link to a named escalation owner with channel + bundle. Recurring escalations (≥3 times) trigger SOP update.

rationale.source: ITIL incident escalation; Faion ops audit

rule.rationale: Unnamed escalation → contractor flies blind OR escalates everything to founder, defeating delegation.

rule.statement: Each step MUST declare a target time budget. Steps exceeding 2× budget flag SOP for review.
rule.rationale: Time budgets surface drift fast; signal of SOP error or hidden complexity.

rule.statement: If preconditions for Service Delivery SOP Template fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying Service Delivery SOP Template outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
